title: PC Cleanup, Bloatware, and Adware Review
description: Review disk pressure, temp files, startup clutter, unwanted apps, browser/proxy adware clues, run safe Windows cleanup after approval, verify, and report.
starter_prompt: Clean up this PC and review bloatware or adware. Start read-only, show findings, use Windows/SAK built-in tools first, do not remove user data or apps without approval, verify afterward, and report.

phases.prompt: Confirm cleanup scope. State that safe temp/component cleanup can be approved in this workflow, but application removal and browser/profile resets require a separate explicit approval for exact targets.
phases.completion: Cleanup scope and safety limits are clear.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; function TrySection($n,[scriptblock]$b){ Section $n; try { & $b } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] ' + $n + ' unavailable: ' + $_.Exception.Message) } }; TrySection 'Volumes' { Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,FileSystem,HealthStatus,OperationalStatus,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List }; TrySection 'Temp folder top-level size' { $paths=@($env:TEMP,(Join-Path $env:windir 'Temp')); foreach($p in $paths){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ $items=Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $sum=($items | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum; [pscustomobject]@{Path=$p; TopLevelItems=$items.Count; TopLevelBytes=[int64]$sum} } } | Format-List }; TrySection 'Component store analysis' { DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /AnalyzeComponentStore }; TrySection 'Large user profile folders sample' { $profile=$env:USERPROFILE; $folders='Desktop','Documents','Downloads','Pictures','Videos','AppData\Local\Temp'; foreach($f in $folders){ $p=Join-Path $profile $f; if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ $items=Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $sum=($items | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum; [pscustomobject]@{Path=$p; TopLevelItems=$items.Count; TopLevelBytes=[int64]$sum} } } | Format-List }

phases.prompt: Collect read-only disk, temp, component store, and high-level user folder evidence. Do not delete files.
phases.completion: Storage cleanup opportunities and risks are captured.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; function TrySection($n,[scriptblock]$b){ Section $n; try { & $b } catch { Write-Output ('[warning] ' + $n + ' unavailable: ' + $_.Exception.Message) } }; TrySection 'Installed desktop apps sample' { Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKLM:\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName } | Select-Object DisplayName,DisplayVersion,Publisher,InstallDate,EstimatedSize,UninstallString | Sort-Object DisplayName | Select-Object -First 240 | Format-List }; TrySection 'Appx packages sample' { Get-AppxPackage -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { -not $_.IsFramework } | Select-Object Name,Publisher,Version,PackageFullName | Sort-Object Name | Select-Object -First 180 | Format-List }; TrySection 'Winget installed apps sample' { winget list --disable-interactivity --accept-source-agreements 2>$null | Select-Object -First 220 }; TrySection 'Startup commands' { Get-CimInstance Win32_StartupCommand -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object Name,Command,Location,User | Format-List }; TrySection 'Enabled non-Microsoft scheduled tasks sample' { Get-ScheduledTask -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.TaskPath -notlike '\Microsoft\*' -and $_.State -ne 'Disabled' } | Select-Object TaskPath,TaskName,State | Sort-Object TaskPath,TaskName | Select-Object -First 180 | Format-Table -AutoSize }; TrySection 'Auto services non-Microsoft sample' { Get-CimInstance Win32_Service -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.StartMode -eq 'Auto' -and $_.PathName -notmatch 'Windows\\System32|Microsoft' } | Select-Object Name,DisplayName,State,StartName,PathName | Select-Object -First 160 | Format-List }; TrySection 'Browser extension folders and policy/proxy clues' { $paths=@((Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Google\Chrome\User Data\Default\Extensions'),(Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA 'Microsoft\Edge\User Data\Default\Extensions'),(Join-Path $env:APPDATA 'Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles')); foreach($p in $paths){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Directory -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object FullName,LastWriteTime | Select-Object -First 120 | Format-List } }; netsh winhttp show proxy; Get-ItemProperty 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ProxyEnable,ProxyServer,AutoConfigURL | Format-List; reg query HKCU\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge /s 2>$null; reg query HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Edge /s 2>$null; reg query HKCU\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome /s 2>$null; reg query HKLM\Software\Policies\Google\Chrome /s 2>$null }

phases.prompt: Collect read-only bloatware/adware evidence: installed apps, Appx packages, package-manager list, startup items, scheduled tasks, auto services, browser extension folders, proxy, and browser policy clues.
phases.completion: Bloatware/adware candidates and startup clutter evidence are captured.

phases.prompt: Analyze cleanup, bloatware, adware, startup, proxy, browser policy, and installed software evidence. Separate safe cleanup actions from app removals that require exact user approval. Flag OEM/security/driver/business apps to preserve by default.
phases.completion: Cleanup findings are prioritized and safe boundaries are explicit.

phases.prompt: Ask human or apply access-mode policy before deleting temp files or running Windows component cleanup. Offer restore point when a cleanup may be hard to roll back. Do not remove apps in this workflow unless the user already gave exact approval; recommend Clean Uninstall for exact app removal.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Disk before cleanup'; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List; Section 'Remove old temp files only'; $cutoff=(Get-Date).AddDays(-7); $targets=@($env:TEMP,(Join-Path $env:windir 'Temp')); foreach($target in $targets){ if(-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $target)){ continue }; Write-Output ('Target: ' + $target); Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $target -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.LastWriteTime -lt $cutoff } | ForEach-Object { try { $path=$_.FullName; Remove-Item -LiteralPath $path -Recurse -Force -ErrorAction Stop; Write-Output ('removed=' + $path) } catch { Write-Output ('skipped=' + $_.FullName + ' reason=' + $_.Exception.Message) } } }; Section 'Start Windows component cleanup scheduled task'; schtasks.exe /Run /TN '\Microsoft\Windows\Servicing\StartComponentCleanup'; Section 'Disk after cleanup request'; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List

phases.prompt: After approval, remove only old files from user/Windows temp folders and trigger the supported Windows StartComponentCleanup task. Do not touch Downloads, documents, browser profiles, app data, or exact app removal targets.
phases.completion: Safe cleanup command output and before/after disk evidence are captured.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output '== Volumes after cleanup =='; Get-Volume | Select-Object DriveLetter,FileSystemLabel,SizeRemaining,Size | Format-List; Write-Output '== Temp folder top-level after cleanup =='; $paths=@($env:TEMP,(Join-Path $env:windir 'Temp')); foreach($p in $paths){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ $items=Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; $sum=($items | Measure-Object Length -Sum).Sum; [pscustomobject]@{Path=$p; TopLevelItems=$items.Count; TopLevelBytes=[int64]$sum} } } | Format-List; Write-Output '== Component cleanup task state =='; schtasks.exe /Query /TN '\Microsoft\Windows\Servicing\StartComponentCleanup' /V /FO LIST

phases.prompt: Verify disk space, temp top-level state, and component cleanup task state after cleanup.
phases.completion: Cleanup effect is verified or remaining cleanup debt is visible.

phases.prompt: Review whether cleanup claims are supported by evidence, whether app-removal recommendations are not overstated, and whether protected user data/app categories were preserved.
phases.completion: Quality review passes or lists gaps.

phases.prompt: Create a technician-readable cleanup handoff report with issue/symptoms, evidence collected, storage findings, bloatware/adware candidates, actions/tools used, what was intentionally not removed, verification results, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and recommended next steps such as exact app uninstall workflow or malware scan.
phases.completion: User has an easy-to-read cleanup report.

phases.prompt: Preserve evidence and reports; remove only temporary scratch files created by this workflow.

acceptance_criteria: Read-only cleanup and adware evidence is collected before deletion.
No user documents, Downloads folder, browser profile, or app data are removed automatically.
Safe cleanup is approval-gated and uses Windows-supported cleanup paths.
Bloatware/adware candidates are recommendations unless exact app removal is approved.
Report clearly says what changed and what still needs user decision.
